title: hieracium albanicum subsp. pivae
intro: Hieracium albanicum subsp. pivae, also known as Albanian Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Hieracium albanicum subsp. pivae can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in folk remedies and traditional herbal practices.
